attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Decision Log — Claude Code Mastery Course
2	
3	**Spec:** `claude-code-mastery`
4	**Purpose:** Persist the *reasoning* behind the requirements so it survives session
5	boundaries. A fresh implementation/maintenance session has none of the original
6	conversation; this file is where the "why" lives. Satisfies R14.AC7.
7	
8	**How to use:** When implementing, if an acceptance criterion seems arbitrary, find its
9	decision here before "fixing" it — the rationale and the rejected alternatives are recorded.
10	When maintaining (CLI changes), check whether a decision's premise still holds.
11	
12	**Status legend:** ✅ decided · 🔓 deliberately deferred to the implementation session (with your input).
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Foundational choices (session 1)
17	
18	| # | Decision | Rationale | Rejected alternatives |
19	|---|---|---|---|
20	| D0.1 | **Deliverable:** Markdown curriculum + hands-on labs against real codebases + reference solutions | Claude Code is terminal-native; competence is forged by doing, not reading | Lessons-only (weak transfer); video/workshop scripts (needs a presenter; out of scope) |
21	| D0.2 | **Spec methodology:** Kiro-style — requirements/design/tasks with EARS + approval gates | Maximum rigor & traceability; the spec itself becomes a teaching artifact (R3.AC2) | Lightweight PRD (looser traceability) |
22	| D0.3 | **Assessment:** capstone-only (sole graded instrument) | One realistic end-to-end proof; units stay instructional, labs are ungraded practice | Per-module grading (rejected by user); self-assessment-only (too weak for a domain novice) |
23	| D0.4 | **Audience:** self-paced solo only | Matches the stated goal (a dev brings *themselves* up to speed); no facilitator overhead | Facilitator overlay / cohort (explicitly out of scope) |
24	
25	## The three structural forks (session 1)
26	
27	| # | Fork | Decision | Why | Ripple |
28	|---|---|---|---|---|
29	| F1 | Organizing axis | **Outcome-driven** — use cases are the spine; features are a *secondary* coverage guarantee | Skills must transfer to real jobs, not be a feature tour; ensure common+powerful cases covered, then guarantee every feature appears ≥1× | Reshaped R2 (spine), R3 (methods), R4 (features→coverage matrix) |
30	| F2 | Breadth vs depth | **Tiered depth for experienced, fast-learning engineers** | Respect a senior dev's time; deep on high-leverage, awareness-level on niche; no SWE basics | Created R5; made R6 template tier-adaptive |
31	| F3 | Versioning | **Version-resilient, NOT pinned** — target the *latest* CLI; quarantine version-specifics; refresh via this spec | User gave "2.1.157" only to signal "use the latest"; the spec must be the instrument that updates the course as the CLI evolves | Rewrote R12 entirely; drives R13 automation, R11 onboarding |
32	
33	> **Note on "2.1.157":** This number was a *signal to target the current/latest CLI*, not a
34	> version to hard-pin. Do not bake it into content. See R12 and `meta/version-record.md`.
35	
36	## Per-requirement decisions (session 2, turn-by-turn review)
37	
38	- **R1 — Non-ranking progress.** Dropped novice/intermediate/elite labels entirely from
39	  learner-facing text: ranking the *person* alienates a capable learner; lower rungs read as
40	  verdicts. Replaced with a **capability map of "can-do" statements** (describe the *work*),
41	  grouped into neutral **stages** (First Wins → Daily Driver → Force Multiplier → Autonomy &
42	  Scale). "Elite" survives only as the internal design target (§1.5). *Rejected:* 2-axis
43	  competency matrix (non-linear, awkward for a single-path course); no-map (loses the rubric anchor).
44	- **R2 — Catalog method, not list.** The *selection method* (frequency × leverage, + capability-map
45	  coverage as secondary) is the requirement; the concrete catalog is a Design deliverable. Added
46	  R2.AC4: use cases must be grounded in real/observed practice, citable — not invented. *Rejected:*
47	  a hybrid "feature primer" up front (sneaks the feature-checklist back in).
48	- **R3 — Workflows subordinate to use cases.** Nine named workflows, each stage-tagged and mapped to
49	  the use case(s) that exercise it (no free-floating lessons). Security review kept bundled with code
50	  review (depth carried by R10). Named set held at nine (didn't expand) to avoid double-listing with
51	  the use-case catalog.
52	- **R4 — Coverage matrix is the single source of truth.** Removed the inline 17-item feature list
53	  (it would rot); the matrix is canonical and seeded in Design. Tiering by frequency × leverage:
54	  high-leverage ⇒ ≥1 hands-on lab; awareness ⇒ ≥1 worked example. *Chose single source of truth over
55	  an informative snapshot* for version resilience.
56	- **R5 — "Basics" carve-out.** "No basics" excludes *general SWE* basics only; AI/Claude-Code concepts
57	  (context windows, non-determinism, agentic loop, verification) are **core content** the persona is new
58	  to. Added per-unit skip-check and split time into reading-time + lab-time.
59	- **R6 — Tier-adaptive template + machine-readable front matter.** Full template for core units, reduced
60	  for awareness units (resolves the rigid-template-vs-tiering tension). Front matter is a lintable schema
61	  (enables R13 automation). Objectives map to can-do statements. Pitfalls (learner mistakes) disambiguated
62	  from R4 anti-patterns (feature misuse) and R10 (safety).
63	- **R7 — Two codebases.** Primary + a small deliberately-messy **legacy** repo, because the "onboard to
64	  unfamiliar code" workflow (R3) is fake against a clean app. Solutions as inspectable git artifacts;
65	  automated verification where feasible; optional BYO clearly marked non-verifiable. *Cost accepted:* one
66	  extra repo in the maintenance surface.
67	- **R8 — Capstone shape (resolved Q3).** Choose-your-own brief from a menu + worked exemplar (realism
68	  without a blank page); optional AI-assisted self-grade the learner must *critique* (dogfoods verify-don't-
69	  trust); structured verification-reflection prompts; optional ungraded mid-course checkpoint that preserves
70	  capstone-only status.
71	- **R9 — Solo survival.** Added a **"when you're stuck" recovery mechanism** (hints, FAQ, using Claude Code
72	  itself) — the defining failure mode of self-paced courses. Added a learner-facing progress checklist from
73	  the capability map. AC2/AC3 made integrative references (DRY in the spec itself).
74	- **R10 — Safety is the thesis.** Dedicated security unit **+** woven reinforcement; hands-on (safety-fenced)
75	  prompt-injection lab; blast-radius/reversibility discipline; third-party extension vetting; **verification
76	  explicitly defined** as more than green tests (read the diff, check the approach, spot-check edges).
77	- **R11 — Lean onboarding.** A `doctor` script (+ manual fallback), a *real* tiny-win first success against the
78	  sample codebase (not hello-world), and a known-good baseline config *established* during onboarding but
79	  *taught* later in its home unit (avoids front-loading). Mapped to First Wins.
80	- **R12 — Version resilience mechanism.** Single machine-readable **version-data file** referenced by key (no
81	  prose duplication); per-fact verification provenance; **automated drift detection**; hard ban on shipping
82	  version-specific claims from model memory; latest-targeting but verified-anchored.
83	- **R13 — Enforcement is required, not optional.** A minimal **required** check suite (front-matter validation,
84	  coverage/map cross-validation, link check, version-data reference integrity) + traceability checks
85	  (automating §8), run as local hooks + CI, themselves worked examples.
86	- **R14 — Authentic dogfooding.** "If we built the course with it, reference it" — but only *authentic*
87	  artifacts, never props. Build case study doubles as capstone exemplar. Light AI-authorship transparency note.
88	- **R15 — Honest accessibility.** CommonMark core with graceful degradation; WSL is the v1 Windows story
89	  (resolved Q4: native Windows deferred); real a11y AC (text equivalents, no color/emoji-only meaning);
90	  Claude Code cost carve-out (it's the prerequisite, not an incidental paid dep).
91	
92	## Provisional inputs captured in session 1 (confirm before relying on them)
93	
94	- **Q1 (partial) ✅ stack chosen:** **Python** for both the primary and legacy sample codebases
95	  (e.g. a small FastAPI service or Python CLI with a pytest suite; seeded bugs/smells per R7.AC2).
96	  Rationale: maximally readable, ubiquitous, light toolchain. *Still open:* the concrete domain.
97	- **Q2 (partial) ✅ size chosen:** **Standard — ~12 units across the 4 stages, ~15–25 hrs**, full
98	  coverage matrix with room for depth on high-leverage features. *Still open:* the concrete use-case
99	  catalog and the concrete capability map.
100	
101	## Design session (2026-05-29) — Q1/Q2 resolved with user
102	
103	| # | Decision | Rationale | Notes |
104	|---|---|---|---|
105	| Q1 ✅ | **Domain: task/project tracker.** Primary `taskflow-api` (FastAPI + SQLModel/SQLite, JWT auth, REST CRUD, pytest, ~2–4k LOC). Legacy `taskflow-cli` (argparse, god-module, globals, dead code, no tests; seeded bugs/smells). | Most common web-app patterns ⇒ maximally transferable labs; rich natural seams for refactor/debug/onboarding (R3); the two repos share a domain so the legacy repo reads as a believable predecessor. Satisfies R7.AC2 criteria. | Concrete file layout + seeded-bug inventory ⇒ design §7 / P4. |
106	| Q2-method ✅ | **Production approach: draft-then-review.** Claude drafts the full use-case catalog + capability map + coverage matrix; user approves/edits before the rest of Design is filled. | User stays the approver (honors phase gates) without per-step latency. | Catalog/map content itself is the deliverable under review — drafted in design §1/§2, not pre-decided here. |
107	| Q2-content ✅ | **Catalog + capability map APPROVED** (user, 2026-05-29). 16 units, can-do statements C1–C17 + cross-cutting CV. Written into design §1/§2/§4. | Selected by frequency×leverage then coverage (R2.AC2); all 9 R3 workflows mapped. | See design.md §1/§2. |
108	| Q2-size ✅ | **Unit count is content-driven, NOT pinned.** The earlier "Standard ~12 units" is **relaxed** by the user → **16 units**, est. ~15–25 hrs (units get tighter, not the course longer). | User: "don't assume a fix limit of 12 … allow the number of units to match the content." | Supersedes the session-1 provisional "~12". |
109	| D-sec ✅ | **Security unit pulled forward to U3** (from the originally-proposed U8). | User directive; establishes guardrails before the heavier Daily-Driver/Force-Multiplier work. Woven verification still runs from U1 (R10.AC7). | design §2, U3. |
110	| D-onb ✅ | **Light/deep onboarding split kept** (Claude's judgement, user-delegated): U2 light exploration on primary repo (First Wins); U9 deep onboarding+refactor on legacy repo (Force Multiplier). | Legacy substrate's authentic complexity is used where it matters; mapping-for-refactor is a distinct higher-leverage capability. Reconciles R3.AC5/AC6. | design §2. |
111	| VR ✅ | **Verified CLI version: 2.1.158** (`claude --version`, 2026-05-29). | Supersedes the "2.1.157" signal per F3 (latest-targeting, not pinned). Anchors `meta/version-record.md`. | Re-verify per-fact at authoring time (R12.AC3). |
112	
113	## Design authored (2026-05-29) — §3–§10 + capstone
114	
115	Workflow stage tags **confirmed** (design §3 — provisional R3.AC1 tags held, no changes). Version
116	architecture (§5: `version-data.yaml` schema, `{{vd:key}}` reference token, callout convention,
117	`version-record` @ 2.1.158), unit model + lintable front-matter schema + tier-adaptive templates +
118	dependency graph (§6), **capstone** brief-menu/exemplar/self-rubric (§6.5), lab & codebase
119	architecture (§7: `taskflow-api`/`taskflow-cli` layout, `start/`+`solution/` branch convention,
120	verify/reset tooling, SEEDED.md), tooling/enforcement suite (§8), repo structure **adopted** (§9,
121	user-delegated), and dogfooding inventory (§10) all authored. Traceability table (§11) fully
122	populated — every R1–R15 ✅.
123	
124	## Design APPROVED (2026-05-29)
125	
126	User reviewed design §0–§11 (focused review of dependency graph, codebase scope, `{{vd:key}}`
127	mechanism, coverage tiering, capstone briefs, tooling stack) and approved **as-is** ("all fine").
128	The six discretionary calls surfaced for review were all accepted: strict numeric default path with
129	U3-before-Daily-Driver and U7-before-U9 prereqs; `taskflow-api` ~2–4k LOC + `taskflow-cli` ~800-line
130	god-module (defects on lab branches, `main` green); custom `{{vd:key}}` token; extended thinking +
131	IDE + output-styles + enterprise settings at awareness tier; three-brief capstone menu; Python
132	`tools/` checks wired as Claude Code hooks + git pre-commit + **GitHub Actions** CI. → **Phase 2
133	gate passed; Phase 3 (Tasks) authoring unblocked.**
134	
135	## Open decisions — 🔓 remaining (deferred to implementation, not blocking)
136	
137	- Per-feature awareness/core tier edge cases (coverage matrix rows 10, 27–29) — seeded in §4; revisit if a unit needs more depth.
138	- Concrete seeded-defect list for `taskflow-cli` + per-lab defects — authored in P4 (`codebases/SEEDED.md`).
139	- Final capstone brief wording (≥3 briefs) — refined in P6 from the §6.5 menu.
140

**Spec:** `claude-code-mastery`
**Purpose:** Persist the *reasoning* behind the requirements so it survives session
boundaries. A fresh implementation/maintenance session has none of the original
conversation; this file is where the "why" lives. Satisfies R14.AC7.

**How to use:** When implementing, if an acceptance criterion seems arbitrary, find its
decision here before "fixing" it — the rationale and the rejected alternatives are recorded.
When maintaining (CLI changes), check whether a decision's premise still holds.

**Status legend:** ✅ decided · 🔓 deliberately deferred to the implementation session (with your input).

---

## Foundational choices (session 1)

| # | Decision | Rationale | Rejected alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| D0.1 | **Deliverable:** Markdown curriculum + hands-on labs against real codebases + reference solutions | Claude Code is terminal-native; competence is forged by doing, not reading | Lessons-only (weak transfer); video/workshop scripts (needs a presenter; out of scope) |
| D0.2 | **Spec methodology:** Kiro-style — requirements/design/tasks with EARS + approval gates | Maximum rigor & traceability; the spec itself becomes a teaching artifact (R3.AC2) | Lightweight PRD (looser traceability) |
| D0.3 | **Assessment:** capstone-only (sole graded instrument) | One realistic end-to-end proof; units stay instructional, labs are ungraded practice | Per-module grading (rejected by user); self-assessment-only (too weak for a domain novice) |
| D0.4 | **Audience:** self-paced solo only | Matches the stated goal (a dev brings *themselves* up to speed); no facilitator overhead | Facilitator overlay / cohort (explicitly out of scope) |

## The three structural forks (session 1)

| # | Fork | Decision | Why | Ripple |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Organizing axis | **Outcome-driven** — use cases are the spine; features are a *secondary* coverage guarantee | Skills must transfer to real jobs, not be a feature tour; ensure common+powerful cases covered, then guarantee every feature appears ≥1× | Reshaped R2 (spine), R3 (methods), R4 (features→coverage matrix) |
| F2 | Breadth vs depth | **Tiered depth for experienced, fast-learning engineers** | Respect a senior dev's time; deep on high-leverage, awareness-level on niche; no SWE basics | Created R5; made R6 template tier-adaptive |
| F3 | Versioning | **Version-resilient, NOT pinned** — target the *latest* CLI; quarantine version-specifics; refresh via this spec | User gave "2.1.157" only to signal "use the latest"; the spec must be the instrument that updates the course as the CLI evolves | Rewrote R12 entirely; drives R13 automation, R11 onboarding |

> **Note on "2.1.157":** This number was a *signal to target the current/latest CLI*, not a
> version to hard-pin. Do not bake it into content. See R12 and `meta/version-record.md`.

## Per-requirement decisions (session 2, turn-by-turn review)

- **R1 — Non-ranking progress.** Dropped novice/intermediate/elite labels entirely from
  learner-facing text: ranking the *person* alienates a capable learner; lower rungs read as
  verdicts. Replaced with a **capability map of "can-do" statements** (describe the *work*),
  grouped into neutral **stages** (First Wins → Daily Driver → Force Multiplier → Autonomy &
  Scale). "Elite" survives only as the internal design target (§1.5). *Rejected:* 2-axis
  competency matrix (non-linear, awkward for a single-path course); no-map (loses the rubric anchor).
- **R2 — Catalog method, not list.** The *selection method* (frequency × leverage, + capability-map
  coverage as secondary) is the requirement; the concrete catalog is a Design deliverable. Added
  R2.AC4: use cases must be grounded in real/observed practice, citable — not invented. *Rejected:*
  a hybrid "feature primer" up front (sneaks the feature-checklist back in).
- **R3 — Workflows subordinate to use cases.** Nine named workflows, each stage-tagged and mapped to
  the use case(s) that exercise it (no free-floating lessons). Security review kept bundled with code
  review (depth carried by R10). Named set held at nine (didn't expand) to avoid double-listing with
  the use-case catalog.
- **R4 — Coverage matrix is the single source of truth.** Removed the inline 17-item feature list
  (it would rot); the matrix is canonical and seeded in Design. Tiering by frequency × leverage:
  high-leverage ⇒ ≥1 hands-on lab; awareness ⇒ ≥1 worked example. *Chose single source of truth over
  an informative snapshot* for version resilience.
- **R5 — "Basics" carve-out.** "No basics" excludes *general SWE* basics only; AI/Claude-Code concepts
  (context windows, non-determinism, agentic loop, verification) are **core content** the persona is new
  to. Added per-unit skip-check and split time into reading-time + lab-time.
- **R6 — Tier-adaptive template + machine-readable front matter.** Full template for core units, reduced
  for awareness units (resolves the rigid-template-vs-tiering tension). Front matter is a lintable schema
  (enables R13 automation). Objectives map to can-do statements. Pitfalls (learner mistakes) disambiguated
  from R4 anti-patterns (feature misuse) and R10 (safety).
- **R7 — Two codebases.** Primary + a small deliberately-messy **legacy** repo, because the "onboard to
  unfamiliar code" workflow (R3) is fake against a clean app. Solutions as inspectable git artifacts;
  automated verification where feasible; optional BYO clearly marked non-verifiable. *Cost accepted:* one
  extra repo in the maintenance surface.
- **R8 — Capstone shape (resolved Q3).** Choose-your-own brief from a menu + worked exemplar (realism
  without a blank page); optional AI-assisted self-grade the learner must *critique* (dogfoods verify-don't-
  trust); structured verification-reflection prompts; optional ungraded mid-course checkpoint that preserves
  capstone-only status.
- **R9 — Solo survival.** Added a **"when you're stuck" recovery mechanism** (hints, FAQ, using Claude Code
  itself) — the defining failure mode of self-paced courses. Added a learner-facing progress checklist from
  the capability map. AC2/AC3 made integrative references (DRY in the spec itself).
- **R10 — Safety is the thesis.** Dedicated security unit **+** woven reinforcement; hands-on (safety-fenced)
  prompt-injection lab; blast-radius/reversibility discipline; third-party extension vetting; **verification
  explicitly defined** as more than green tests (read the diff, check the approach, spot-check edges).
- **R11 — Lean onboarding.** A `doctor` script (+ manual fallback), a *real* tiny-win first success against the
  sample codebase (not hello-world), and a known-good baseline config *established* during onboarding but
  *taught* later in its home unit (avoids front-loading). Mapped to First Wins.
- **R12 — Version resilience mechanism.** Single machine-readable **version-data file** referenced by key (no
  prose duplication); per-fact verification provenance; **automated drift detection**; hard ban on shipping
  version-specific claims from model memory; latest-targeting but verified-anchored.
- **R13 — Enforcement is required, not optional.** A minimal **required** check suite (front-matter validation,
  coverage/map cross-validation, link check, version-data reference integrity) + traceability checks
  (automating §8), run as local hooks + CI, themselves worked examples.
- **R14 — Authentic dogfooding.** "If we built the course with it, reference it" — but only *authentic*
  artifacts, never props. Build case study doubles as capstone exemplar. Light AI-authorship transparency note.
- **R15 — Honest accessibility.** CommonMark core with graceful degradation; WSL is the v1 Windows story
  (resolved Q4: native Windows deferred); real a11y AC (text equivalents, no color/emoji-only meaning);
  Claude Code cost carve-out (it's the prerequisite, not an incidental paid dep).

## Provisional inputs captured in session 1 (confirm before relying on them)

- **Q1 (partial) ✅ stack chosen:** **Python** for both the primary and legacy sample codebases
  (e.g. a small FastAPI service or Python CLI with a pytest suite; seeded bugs/smells per R7.AC2).
  Rationale: maximally readable, ubiquitous, light toolchain. *Still open:* the concrete domain.
- **Q2 (partial) ✅ size chosen:** **Standard — ~12 units across the 4 stages, ~15–25 hrs**, full
  coverage matrix with room for depth on high-leverage features. *Still open:* the concrete use-case
  catalog and the concrete capability map.

## Design session (2026-05-29) — Q1/Q2 resolved with user

| # | Decision | Rationale | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 ✅ | **Domain: task/project tracker.** Primary `taskflow-api` (FastAPI + SQLModel/SQLite, JWT auth, REST CRUD, pytest, ~2–4k LOC). Legacy `taskflow-cli` (argparse, god-module, globals, dead code, no tests; seeded bugs/smells). | Most common web-app patterns ⇒ maximally transferable labs; rich natural seams for refactor/debug/onboarding (R3); the two repos share a domain so the legacy repo reads as a believable predecessor. Satisfies R7.AC2 criteria. | Concrete file layout + seeded-bug inventory ⇒ design §7 / P4. |
| Q2-method ✅ | **Production approach: draft-then-review.** Claude drafts the full use-case catalog + capability map + coverage matrix; user approves/edits before the rest of Design is filled. | User stays the approver (honors phase gates) without per-step latency. | Catalog/map content itself is the deliverable under review — drafted in design §1/§2, not pre-decided here. |
| Q2-content ✅ | **Catalog + capability map APPROVED** (user, 2026-05-29). 16 units, can-do statements C1–C17 + cross-cutting CV. Written into design §1/§2/§4. | Selected by frequency×leverage then coverage (R2.AC2); all 9 R3 workflows mapped. | See design.md §1/§2. |
| Q2-size ✅ | **Unit count is content-driven, NOT pinned.** The earlier "Standard ~12 units" is **relaxed** by the user → **16 units**, est. ~15–25 hrs (units get tighter, not the course longer). | User: "don't assume a fix limit of 12 … allow the number of units to match the content." | Supersedes the session-1 provisional "~12". |
| D-sec ✅ | **Security unit pulled forward to U3** (from the originally-proposed U8). | User directive; establishes guardrails before the heavier Daily-Driver/Force-Multiplier work. Woven verification still runs from U1 (R10.AC7). | design §2, U3. |
| D-onb ✅ | **Light/deep onboarding split kept** (Claude's judgement, user-delegated): U2 light exploration on primary repo (First Wins); U9 deep onboarding+refactor on legacy repo (Force Multiplier). | Legacy substrate's authentic complexity is used where it matters; mapping-for-refactor is a distinct higher-leverage capability. Reconciles R3.AC5/AC6. | design §2. |
| VR ✅ | **Verified CLI version: 2.1.158** (`claude --version`, 2026-05-29). | Supersedes the "2.1.157" signal per F3 (latest-targeting, not pinned). Anchors `meta/version-record.md`. | Re-verify per-fact at authoring time (R12.AC3). |

## Design authored (2026-05-29) — §3–§10 + capstone

Workflow stage tags **confirmed** (design §3 — provisional R3.AC1 tags held, no changes). Version
architecture (§5: `version-data.yaml` schema, `{{vd:key}}` reference token, callout convention,
`version-record` @ 2.1.158), unit model + lintable front-matter schema + tier-adaptive templates +
dependency graph (§6), **capstone** brief-menu/exemplar/self-rubric (§6.5), lab & codebase
architecture (§7: `taskflow-api`/`taskflow-cli` layout, `start/`+`solution/` branch convention,
verify/reset tooling, SEEDED.md), tooling/enforcement suite (§8), repo structure **adopted** (§9,
user-delegated), and dogfooding inventory (§10) all authored. Traceability table (§11) fully
populated — every R1–R15 ✅.

## Design APPROVED (2026-05-29)

User reviewed design §0–§11 (focused review of dependency graph, codebase scope, `{{vd:key}}`
mechanism, coverage tiering, capstone briefs, tooling stack) and approved **as-is** ("all fine").
The six discretionary calls surfaced for review were all accepted: strict numeric default path with
U3-before-Daily-Driver and U7-before-U9 prereqs; `taskflow-api` ~2–4k LOC + `taskflow-cli` ~800-line
god-module (defects on lab branches, `main` green); custom `{{vd:key}}` token; extended thinking +
IDE + output-styles + enterprise settings at awareness tier; three-brief capstone menu; Python
`tools/` checks wired as Claude Code hooks + git pre-commit + **GitHub Actions** CI. → **Phase 2
gate passed; Phase 3 (Tasks) authoring unblocked.**

## Open decisions — 🔓 remaining (deferred to implementation, not blocking)

- Per-feature awareness/core tier edge cases (coverage matrix rows 10, 27–29) — seeded in §4; revisit if a unit needs more depth.
- Concrete seeded-defect list for `taskflow-cli` + per-lab defects — authored in P4 (`codebases/SEEDED.md`).
- Final capstone brief wording (≥3 briefs) — refined in P6 from the §6.5 menu.

5. Stop at the Definition of Done in `IMPLEMENTATION.md` §6; run the enforcement + traceability checks.

1	# P2 — Scaffolding & cross-cutting artifacts
2	
3	**Goal:** materialize the repo skeleton and the single-source machine-readable `meta/*` artifacts
4	**from** the approved design, so P3 tooling has something to validate and P5 units have something to
5	reference. Nothing here is invented — each file is generated from the cited design section.
6	
7	**Inputs:** `design.md` §1, §2, §3, §4, §5, §6, §9 · **Verify version facts against CLI 2.1.158** (R12.AC3).
8	
9	## Tasks
10	
11	### 2.1 Repo skeleton (design §9)
12	- [ ] Create the directory layout from design §9 (`course/`, `codebases/{primary,legacy}/`, `meta/`,
13	      `meta/templates/`, `tools/`, `.claude/`, `.github/workflows/`). [R13.AC1]
14	- [ ] `meta/conventions.md` — naming/style conventions (units `NN-slug`, `start/uNN-labM` +
15	      `solution/uNN-labM`, kebab-case meta/tools, `{{vd:key}}`). [R13.AC1]
16	- [ ] `meta/glossary.md` — terms from requirements §2, centralized & referenced (not duplicated). [R13.AC2]
17	
18	### 2.2 Capability map (design §1)
19	- [ ] `meta/capability-map.yaml` — C1–C17 + CV, each `{id, statement, stage, home_unit}`. [R1]
20	- [ ] Generate `meta/capability-map.json` (tooling reads this). [R13.AC2]
21	
22	### 2.3 Use-case catalog (design §2)
23	- [ ] `meta/use-case-catalog.yaml` — U1–U16, each `{unit, stage, job, success, workflows, features,
24	      grounding, advances, depth_tier: core}` + recorded prioritization rationale. [R2.AC2/AC4]
25	
26	### 2.4 Workflows (design §3)
27	- [ ] `meta/workflows.md` — W1–W9 table (decision criterion, verification, stage, units) **plus** the
28	      generalized pattern write-up per workflow; units reference this, don't re-explain. [R3, R5.AC5]
29	
30	### 2.5 Coverage matrix (design §4)
31	- [ ] `meta/coverage-matrix.yaml` — 29 areas, each `{id, area, tier, covered_by, version_data_key}`;
32	      intentional-exclusion/awareness notes recorded. [R4.AC1/AC4/AC5]
33	
34	### 2.6 Version layer (design §5)
35	- [ ] `meta/version-data.yaml` — seed one entry per `vd:*` key referenced in the coverage matrix;
36	      **verify each value against the installed CLI** (`--help`/`/help`/docs) and record `provenance`,
37	      `verified_version: 2.1.158`, `verified_date`; mark anything unverifiable `unverified: true`. [R12.AC2/AC3/AC4]
38	- [ ] Generate `meta/version-data.json`.
39	- [ ] `meta/version-record.md` — table seeded at **2.1.158 / 2026-05-29 / method**. [R12.AC5]
40	
41	### 2.7 Unit model (design §6)
42	- [ ] `meta/unit-frontmatter.schema.json` — JSON Schema for unit front matter (all keys from §6). [R6.AC3]
43	- [ ] `meta/templates/unit-core.md` + `meta/templates/unit-awareness.md` — the two ordered templates. [R6.AC1/AC2]
44	
45	### 2.8 Dogfooding + learner-facing scaffolds
46	- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` (repo root) — project memory; **authentic** (used to build the course). [R14.AC1]
47	- [ ] `course/progress-checklist.md` — **generated from** `capability-map` (do not hand-maintain). [R9.AC5]
48	- [ ] `course/stuck.md` — "when you're stuck": hints-vs-solutions, FAQ, using Claude Code to get unstuck. [R9.AC4]
49	- [ ] Top-level `README.md` (learner-facing) — entry capabilities assumed + full can-do set delivered. [R1.AC6]
50	
51	## Definition of Done (P2)
52	- [ ] Every `meta/*` artifact is machine-readable and consistent with its design section. [R13.AC2]
53	- [ ] `capability-map` ↔ `use-case-catalog` ↔ `coverage-matrix` cross-reference cleanly (every C-id has

55	- [ ] No version-specific value authored from memory; all carry provenance or `unverified`. [R12.AC3]

# P2 — Scaffolding & cross-cutting artifacts

**Goal:** materialize the repo skeleton and the single-source machine-readable `meta/*` artifacts
**from** the approved design, so P3 tooling has something to validate and P5 units have something to
reference. Nothing here is invented — each file is generated from the cited design section.

**Inputs:** `design.md` §1, §2, §3, §4, §5, §6, §9 · **Verify version facts against CLI 2.1.158** (R12.AC3).

## Tasks

### 2.1 Repo skeleton (design §9)
- [ ] Create the directory layout from design §9 (`course/`, `codebases/{primary,legacy}/`, `meta/`,
      `meta/templates/`, `tools/`, `.claude/`, `.github/workflows/`). [R13.AC1]
- [ ] `meta/conventions.md` — naming/style conventions (units `NN-slug`, `start/uNN-labM` +
      `solution/uNN-labM`, kebab-case meta/tools, `{{vd:key}}`). [R13.AC1]
- [ ] `meta/glossary.md` — terms from requirements §2, centralized & referenced (not duplicated). [R13.AC2]

### 2.2 Capability map (design §1)
- [ ] `meta/capability-map.yaml` — C1–C17 + CV, each `{id, statement, stage, home_unit}`. [R1]
- [ ] Generate `meta/capability-map.json` (tooling reads this). [R13.AC2]

### 2.3 Use-case catalog (design §2)
- [ ] `meta/use-case-catalog.yaml` — U1–U16, each `{unit, stage, job, success, workflows, features,
      grounding, advances, depth_tier: core}` + recorded prioritization rationale. [R2.AC2/AC4]

### 2.4 Workflows (design §3)
- [ ] `meta/workflows.md` — W1–W9 table (decision criterion, verification, stage, units) **plus** the
      generalized pattern write-up per workflow; units reference this, don't re-explain. [R3, R5.AC5]

### 2.5 Coverage matrix (design §4)
- [ ] `meta/coverage-matrix.yaml` — 29 areas, each `{id, area, tier, covered_by, version_data_key}`;
      intentional-exclusion/awareness notes recorded. [R4.AC1/AC4/AC5]

### 2.6 Version layer (design §5)
- [ ] `meta/version-data.yaml` — seed one entry per `vd:*` key referenced in the coverage matrix;
      **verify each value against the installed CLI** (`--help`/`/help`/docs) and record `provenance`,
      `verified_version: 2.1.158`, `verified_date`; mark anything unverifiable `unverified: true`. [R12.AC2/AC3/AC4]
- [ ] Generate `meta/version-data.json`.
- [ ] `meta/version-record.md` — table seeded at **2.1.158 / 2026-05-29 / method**. [R12.AC5]

### 2.7 Unit model (design §6)
- [ ] `meta/unit-frontmatter.schema.json` — JSON Schema for unit front matter (all keys from §6). [R6.AC3]
- [ ] `meta/templates/unit-core.md` + `meta/templates/unit-awareness.md` — the two ordered templates. [R6.AC1/AC2]

### 2.8 Dogfooding + learner-facing scaffolds
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` (repo root) — project memory; **authentic** (used to build the course). [R14.AC1]
- [ ] `course/progress-checklist.md` — **generated from** `capability-map` (do not hand-maintain). [R9.AC5]
- [ ] `course/stuck.md` — "when you're stuck": hints-vs-solutions, FAQ, using Claude Code to get unstuck. [R9.AC4]
- [ ] Top-level `README.md` (learner-facing) — entry capabilities assumed + full can-do set delivered. [R1.AC6]

## Definition of Done (P2)
- [ ] Every `meta/*` artifact is machine-readable and consistent with its design section. [R13.AC2]
- [ ] `capability-map` ↔ `use-case-catalog` ↔ `coverage-matrix` cross-reference cleanly (every C-id has
      a home unit; every area has a covering unit) — will be enforced by P3 `check-coverage`.
- [ ] No version-specific value authored from memory; all carry provenance or `unverified`. [R12.AC3]

2

11

13	AI-assisted coding to elite Claude Code practitioner. It is **outcome-driven** (organized around
14	real use cases, not a feature tour), **tiered** for fast-learning senior engineers, and
15	**version-resilient** (targets the latest CLI; built to be refreshed as the tool changes — using
16	this spec). Assessment is a single capstone. See [`requirements.md`](./requirements.md) §1.

18	## 2. Read order (do this first, every fresh session)

20	1. **This file** — orientation + how to work within context limits.
21	2. [`decisions.md`](./decisions.md) — *why* the spec is the way it is; the rejected alternatives.
22	   Read before "improving" any requirement.

27	The persistent project memory (auto-loaded each session) also points here. Trust the **files**
28	over memory for specifics; memory is a pointer, the spec is the source of truth.

30	## 3. Current state
31	
32	| Phase | File | Status |
33	|---|---|---|
34	| 1. Requirements | `requirements.md` | ✅ **APPROVED** (2026-05-29) — reviewed turn-by-turn, internally consistent, gap-free IDs |
35	| 2. Design | `design.md` | ✅ **Authored** (2026-05-29) — Q1/Q2 resolved; §0–§11 complete (capability map, catalog, workflows, coverage matrix, version arch, unit model + capstone, labs, tooling, repo, dogfooding, traceability) |
36	| 3. Tasks | `tasks.md` | 🟨 **Planned** (2026-05-29) — detailed per-phase task files `tasks/P1–P6*.md` authored & traced; P1 ✅; build execution P2→P6 not started |
37	| — | `decisions.md` | ✅ Rationale captured (incl. Design-session Q1/Q2 resolutions) |

39	## 4. Q1/Q2 — ✅ RESOLVED with the user (2026-05-29)
40	
41	Both blocking inputs are now decided (full record in `decisions.md` → "Design session"):
42	
43	- **Q1 ✅** — domain = **task/project tracker**; primary `taskflow-api` (FastAPI + SQLModel + pytest),
44	  legacy `taskflow-cli` (messy, untested). Concrete layout/seeded-bug inventory → design §7 / P4.

48	**Remaining open** (don't guess; see `decisions.md` → Open decisions): repo structure (§9),
49	per-feature awareness/core edge cases, capstone brief menu (R8). Next Design work: §3 (workflows),
50	§5 (version architecture), §6 (unit model/schema/templates), §7 (labs/codebases), §8–§10.
51	
52	## 5. Context-management protocol (important — this is a large build)
53	
54	The full course will exceed any single context window. Work in **bounded slices**:
55	
56	- **Don't load everything.** For a given task, load: `requirements.md` (or just the relevant Rs),
57	  the relevant `design.md` section, and the one `tasks/<section>.md` you're executing. Skip the rest.
58	- **One unit at a time.** Authoring is per-unit. Each unit is self-contained (R6.AC5), so you can

60	  it references — not the other units.

64	  and append to `decisions.md` when you make a non-obvious call. The next session reads these, not your memory.
65	- **Verify version-specific details against the installed CLI** (`--help`, `/help`, docs) — never from
66	  model memory (R12.AC3, hard rule). Record provenance (R12.AC4).

87	version-specific details against the installed CLI → update **only** `meta/version-data.*` and any

# Start Here — Implementation Guide

**Spec:** `claude-code-mastery`
**Audience:** a Claude Code session (and its human) picking up this spec to build or maintain
the course. **You likely have no memory of how this spec was written** — this document and
[`decisions.md`](./decisions.md) are how that context is restored.

---

## 1. What this project is (in one paragraph)

A self-paced, solo, hands-on **course** that takes a seasoned developer who is new to
AI-assisted coding to elite Claude Code practitioner. It is **outcome-driven** (organized around
real use cases, not a feature tour), **tiered** for fast-learning senior engineers, and
**version-resilient** (targets the latest CLI; built to be refreshed as the tool changes — using
this spec). Assessment is a single capstone. See [`requirements.md`](./requirements.md) §1.

## 2. Read order (do this first, every fresh session)

1. **This file** — orientation + how to work within context limits.
2. [`decisions.md`](./decisions.md) — *why* the spec is the way it is; the rejected alternatives.
   Read before "improving" any requirement.
3. [`requirements.md`](./requirements.md) — the authoritative WHAT (R1–R15, EARS). Stable IDs.
4. [`design.md`](./design.md) — the HOW (architecture, schemas, artifact inventory, traceability).
5. [`tasks.md`](./tasks.md) — the ordered, chunked build plan.

The persistent project memory (auto-loaded each session) also points here. Trust the **files**
over memory for specifics; memory is a pointer, the spec is the source of truth.

## 3. Current state

| Phase | File | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Requirements | `requirements.md` | ✅ **APPROVED** (2026-05-29) — reviewed turn-by-turn, internally consistent, gap-free IDs |
| 2. Design | `design.md` | ✅ **Authored** (2026-05-29) — Q1/Q2 resolved; §0–§11 complete (capability map, catalog, workflows, coverage matrix, version arch, unit model + capstone, labs, tooling, repo, dogfooding, traceability) |
| 3. Tasks | `tasks.md` | 🟨 **Planned** (2026-05-29) — detailed per-phase task files `tasks/P1–P6*.md` authored & traced; P1 ✅; build execution P2→P6 not started |
| — | `decisions.md` | ✅ Rationale captured (incl. Design-session Q1/Q2 resolutions) |

## 4. Q1/Q2 — ✅ RESOLVED with the user (2026-05-29)

Both blocking inputs are now decided (full record in `decisions.md` → "Design session"):

- **Q1 ✅** — domain = **task/project tracker**; primary `taskflow-api` (FastAPI + SQLModel + pytest),
  legacy `taskflow-cli` (messy, untested). Concrete layout/seeded-bug inventory → design §7 / P4.
- **Q2 ✅** — capability map (C1–C17 + CV) and 16-unit use-case catalog **approved** (design §1/§2/§4).
  Unit count is **content-driven** (the "~12" was relaxed); security pulled forward to U3.

**Remaining open** (don't guess; see `decisions.md` → Open decisions): repo structure (§9),
per-feature awareness/core edge cases, capstone brief menu (R8). Next Design work: §3 (workflows),
§5 (version architecture), §6 (unit model/schema/templates), §7 (labs/codebases), §8–§10.

## 5. Context-management protocol (important — this is a large build)

The full course will exceed any single context window. Work in **bounded slices**:

- **Don't load everything.** For a given task, load: `requirements.md` (or just the relevant Rs),
  the relevant `design.md` section, and the one `tasks/<section>.md` you're executing. Skip the rest.
- **One unit at a time.** Authoring is per-unit. Each unit is self-contained (R6.AC5), so you can
  build unit N loading only its task file, its front-matter schema, and the cross-cutting artifacts
  it references — not the other units.
- **Cross-cutting facts live in single files** (`meta/…`), referenced by key, never duplicated
  (R13.AC2, R12.AC2). Read the one you need; don't inline its contents elsewhere.
- **Update state as you go.** Check off tasks in `tasks.md`/`tasks/*`, bump `meta/version-record.md`,
  and append to `decisions.md` when you make a non-obvious call. The next session reads these, not your memory.
- **Verify version-specific details against the installed CLI** (`--help`, `/help`, docs) — never from
  model memory (R12.AC3, hard rule). Record provenance (R12.AC4).

## 6. Definition of Done for the build (v1)

Implementation is complete when **all** hold:

1. Every requirement R1–R15 is satisfied and referenced by ≥1 artifact (traceability check passes, R13.AC5).
2. Every can-do statement traces to ≥1 lab **and** ≥1 capstone-rubric dimension (R1.AC5, R13.AC5).
3. Every capability area in the coverage matrix is covered at its assigned tier (R4.AC2; high-leverage ⇒ lab).
4. Every unit conforms to the tier-appropriate template with valid machine-readable front matter (R6, R13.AC4).
5. Every lab has objective self-check criteria, an inspectable reference solution, and a reset path (R7).
6. The capstone, its brief menu, exemplar, and self-applicable rubric exist (R8).
7. The two sample codebases exist and pass their own test suites (R7.AC1–AC2).
8. The required enforcement suite + traceability checks run green locally and in CI (R13.AC4–AC6).
9. The version-data file, version-record, and drift-detection check exist and pass (R12).
10. Dogfooding artifacts exist and are accurately referenced; the build case study + transparency note exist (R14).
11. The "when you're stuck" recovery resources and the learner progress checklist exist (R9.AC4–AC5).

## 7. Maintenance (when the CLI changes later)

Follow the R12.AC7 refresh process: run the drift-detection check → re-verify the flagged
version-specific details against the installed CLI → update **only** `meta/version-data.*` and any
truly affected locations → bump `meta/version-record.md` → re-run the enforcement suite. Because
version-specifics are quarantined (R12.AC2/AC8), a version bump should touch a bounded set of files,
not the prose.

content.content:
1	# Design — Claude Code Mastery Course
2	
3	**Spec:** `claude-code-mastery`
4	**Phase:** Design (Phase 2 of 3)
5	**Status:** ✅ **AUTHORED** (2026-05-29) — §0–§11 complete; capability map / catalog / coverage matrix
6	user-approved; §3,§5–§10 + capstone (§6.5) authored; traceability table (§11) fully populated.
7	Verified against CLI **2.1.158**. Ready for review → Phase 3 (Tasks).
8	
9	> **Authored.** Each section states its purpose, inputs (requirements + decisions), and the concrete
10	> artifact(s) it produces — now filled with the approved design. The artifacts themselves (the
11	> machine-readable `meta/*` files, templates, tooling, codebases) are **generated in Phase 3 / P2–P6**
12	> from these specifications; the traceability table (§11) maps every R1–R15 to its design component.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 0. Inputs to resolve first (with the user) — ✅ RESOLVED (2026-05-29)
17	
18	- **Q1 ✅** — **Task/project-tracker domain.** Primary `taskflow-api` (FastAPI + SQLModel/SQLite,
19	  JWT auth, REST CRUD, pytest). Legacy `taskflow-cli` (argparse, god-module, no tests; seeded
20	  bugs/smells). Details → §7. Recorded in [`decisions.md`](./decisions.md) → Design session.
21	- **Q2 ✅** — **Capability map + use-case catalog approved** (§1, §2 below). **Unit count is
22	  content-driven, not pinned** (the original "~12" was relaxed by the user): **16 units**, est.
23	  ~15–25 hrs. Security unit **pulled forward to U3**. Light/deep onboarding split **kept** (U2/U9).
24	- **CLI version ✅** — verified **2.1.158** (`claude --version`, 2026-05-29) → anchors
25	  `meta/version-record.md` (§5).
26	
27	---
28	
29	## 1. Capability map  → produces `meta/capability-map.{yaml,json}`  [R1]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
30	
31	The full set of **can-do statements** — outcome-phrased, observable, describing the work and never
32	ranking the person (R1.AC1/AC3) — grouped into the four neutral **stages** (R1.AC2). Each maps to
33	exactly one stage and to its home unit (R1.AC4); each will trace to ≥1 lab **and** ≥1 capstone-rubric
34	dimension (R1.AC5), enforced by the R13.AC5 check once §6/§8 land.
35	
36	| Stage | ID | "I can …" | Home unit |
37	|---|---|---|---|
38	| **First Wins** | C1 | install, authenticate, and confirm a working Claude Code setup with a repeatable check | U1 |
39	| | C2 | explain the agentic loop and make a single, **verified** change to a real codebase | U1 |
40	| | C3 | use Claude to explore and explain an unfamiliar codebase and locate where to make a change | U2 |
41	| | C4 | operate Claude safely — choose permission modes, protect secrets, recognize prompt injection, limit blast radius | U3 |
42	| | C5 | steer Claude with project memory (`CLAUDE.md`) and deliberate context selection | U4 |
43	| **Daily Driver** | C6 | implement a feature end-to-end via explore→plan→code→commit and verify the result myself | U5 |
44	| | C7 | drive a change test-first (confirm a failing test, implement to green) | U6 |
45	| | C8 | diagnose and fix an unfamiliar failure, confirming root cause rather than guessing | U7 |
46	| | C9 | turn a body of work into clean commits and a reviewable PR with an accurate description | U8 |
47	| **Force Multiplier** | C10 | onboard to and execute a large multi-file refactor on a legacy codebase, keeping it green | U9 |
48	| | C11 | run a spec-driven workflow (requirements→design→tasks) against a real spec | U10 |
49	| | C12 | review a change for correctness **and** security with Claude | U11 |
50	| **Autonomy & Scale** | C13 | package repeatable work as custom commands & skills | U12 |
51	| | C14 | delegate focused / parallel sub-tasks to subagents | U13 |
52	| | C15 | automate guardrails and standards with hooks | U14 |
53	| | C16 | connect Claude to external tools/data via MCP and vet third-party extensions before installing | U15 |
54	| | C17 | run Claude headlessly / in CI and coordinate parallel agents via git worktrees | U16 |
55	| **Cross-cutting** | **CV** | verify Claude's output rigorously — read the diff, check the approach, spot-check edges — not merely trust green tests | every lab; consolidated U3 & U11 (R10.AC6) |
56	
57	- **Output:** machine-readable `meta/capability-map.{yaml,json}` (`id`, `statement`, `stage`,
58	  `home_unit`), seeded in P2. The learner-facing progress checklist (R9.AC5) is **generated** from it.
59	- **CV is cross-cutting:** every workflow lab must include at least one explicit verification step
60	  (R10.AC7); CV is "advanced" by every unit and consolidated in U3 (safety) and U11 (review).
61	
62	## 2. Use-case catalog  → produces `meta/use-case-catalog.yaml`  [R2]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
63	
64	Prioritized jobs-to-be-done, one per unit. **Prioritization rationale (R2.AC2):** ordered primarily
65	by *frequency × leverage* (the daily loops a developer hits first and most often lead; rarer/higher-
66	ceiling jobs follow), then adjusted for *capability-map coverage* so the catalog collectively
67	exercises every can-do statement (C1–C17 + CV) and every coverage-matrix area (§4). All entries are
68	grounded in real, documented engineering practice (R2.AC4) — none invented. **Depth tier:** every
69	unit is **core** (each carries ≥1 hands-on lab); awareness-tier coverage is applied at the *feature*
70	level inside the coverage matrix (§4), per R4.AC4 / R5.AC4.
71	
72	| Unit | Stage | Use case (job-to-be-done) | Success outcome | Workflow(s) (R3) / key features | Real-practice grounding (R2.AC4) | Advances |
73	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
74	| **U1** | First Wins | Get set up and make a first **verified** win against `taskflow-api` | doctor passes; one verified one-line change committed | agentic loop, verification intro; REPL, prompting (R11) | Anthropic first-run / onboarding guidance | C1, C2 |
75	| **U2** | First Wins | Understand a codebase I've never seen | learner can explain the app's architecture and locate a change site, unaided | onboarding *(light)*; codebase Q&A, search, `@`-refs | "explore before you change" practice | C3 |
76	| **U3** | First Wins | Operate Claude without leaking secrets or causing damage *(security unit, R10)* | learner observes a prompt-injection attempt in a sandbox and applies a defense; chooses an appropriate permission mode | permissions/modes, secret hygiene, prompt-injection lab, blast-radius, checkpoints | threat-modeling, least-privilege | C4, CV |
77	| **U4** | First Wins | Make Claude reliably follow my project's conventions | a `CLAUDE.md` + context choices measurably change Claude's behavior on a task | context engineering; `CLAUDE.md` memory, `/context` (R11.AC4 home) | context-management best practice | C5 |
78	| **U5** | Daily Driver | Ship a new feature in `taskflow-api` | feature implemented & verified end-to-end via a plan | **explore→plan→code→commit**; plan mode | Anthropic's flagship coding loop | C6 |
79	| **U6** | Daily Driver | Add behavior safely, test-first | a failing test is written/confirmed, then driven to green | **TDD** | classic TDD, AI-adapted | C7 |
80	| **U7** | Daily Driver | A test/endpoint is broken — find and fix it | root cause confirmed (not guessed); fix verified | **debugging an unfamiliar failure** | scientific debugging | C8 |
81	| **U8** | Daily Driver | Turn my work into a clean, reviewable PR | clean commits + accurate PR description; review-ready | **Git/PR workflow**; `gh` | trunk-based / PR-review practice | C9 |
82	| **U9** | Force Mult. | Onboard to the messy `taskflow-cli` and refactor it safely | architecture mapped; multi-file refactor lands with suite green | **onboarding (deep)** + **multi-file refactoring** | legacy-rescue / strangler-fig practice | C10 |
83	| **U10** | Force Mult. | Build a feature spec-first | reqs→design→tasks produced and used to drive the change | **spec-driven dev** — *this very spec is the worked example* (R3.AC2) | spec-/intent-driven development | C11 |
84	| **U11** | Force Mult. | Review a change for correctness **and** security | a review surfaces real correctness + security issues; verification beyond green tests | **code & security review**; `/code-review`, `/security-review` | code-review & threat-review practice | C12, CV |
85	| **U12** | Autonomy | Eliminate repetitive prompting | a reusable custom command and a skill replace a hand-typed routine | custom **slash commands**, **skills** | dogfooded authoring artifacts (R14) | C13 |
86	| **U13** | Autonomy | Delegate focused / parallel sub-tasks | a **subagent** performs a scoped task and returns a usable result | **subagents** | agent-delegation practice | C14 |

100	## 3. Workflow methods  → produces `meta/workflows.md`  [R3]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
101	
102	The nine named workflows (R3.AC1), each with a **decision criterion** (when to choose it over
103	alternatives, R3.AC3), an **expected verification step** (R3.AC3 + CV), its **confirmed stage**, and
104	the **exercising unit(s)** (R3.AC4–AC6). Stage tags match the R3.AC1 provisional set and the §1
105	capability map — no changes needed. Workflows are taught *inside* their use case, then generalized
106	(R3.AC4); none is a free-floating lesson (R3.AC5).
107	
108	| # | Workflow | Stage | When to choose it | Expected verification (CV) | Unit(s) |
109	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
110	| W1 | explore → plan → code → commit | Daily Driver | Default for any non-trivial change | Review the **plan** before coding; read the diff + run tests before commit | U5 |
111	| W2 | test-driven development | Daily Driver | Behavior is specifiable as a test; regression-prone or bug-fix work | Confirm the test fails first **for the right reason** (red), then green; review the impl | U6 |
112	| W3 | debugging an unfamiliar failure | Daily Driver | Something is broken and the cause is unknown | Reproduce first; capture the bug in a failing test; confirm root cause, then that the fix doesn't regress | U7 |
113	| W4 | Git / PR workflow | Daily Driver | Turning work into shareable history/review | Review the staged diff; ensure message matches change; self-review the PR | U8 |
114	| W5 | multi-file refactoring | Force Multiplier | Cross-cutting change touching many files; rename/restructure | Behavior-preserving: full suite green **before and after**; diff-review for scope creep | U9 |
115	| W6 | code & security review | Force Multiplier | Before merging a significant or security-sensitive change | The workflow *is* verification — but cross-check findings, don't trust the review blindly (CV) | U11 |
116	| W7 | spec-driven development | Force Multiplier | Large/ambiguous feature where upfront alignment pays off | Each phase gate reviewed/approved before the next; trace requirements→design→tasks | U10 |
117	| W8 | onboarding to an unfamiliar/large codebase | First Wins *(light)* → Force Multiplier *(deep)* | Entering code you don't know | Validate Claude's architecture summary against the actual code (spot-check claims) | U2 (light), U9 (deep) |
118	| W9 | running parallel agents (git worktrees) | Autonomy & Scale | Multiple independent tasks that can run concurrently | Isolate each in a worktree; review each agent's diff independently before integrating | U16 |
119	
120	- **R3.AC2 (spec-driven uses this spec):** W7/U10 uses **this very `specs/claude-code-mastery/`**
121	  (requirements → design → tasks, with approval gates) as its worked example.
122	- **R3.AC6 reconciliation:** W8 is intentionally two-tiered (light U2 / deep U9 — see §2); every
123	  workflow has ≥1 exercising unit, and no catalog row is better expressed as a workflow than a use case.
124	- Output: `meta/workflows.md` (this table + the generalized pattern write-up per workflow), referenced
125	  by the units rather than re-explained (R5.AC5).

172	## 5. Version-resilience architecture  → produces `meta/version-data.*` + `meta/version-record.md`  [R12]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
173	
174	**Version-data file** — `meta/version-data.yaml` (authoritative; `meta/version-data.json` generated for
175	tooling). Single store of every version-specific detail, referenced **by key**, never duplicated in
176	prose (R12.AC2/AC8). Schema per entry:
177	
178	```yaml
179	permission-modes:                 # the key prose references
180	  kind: feature                   # command | flag | path | setting | feature | availability
181	  value: "default, acceptEdits, plan, bypassPermissions"
182	  provenance: "claude --help / docs"   # how it was checked (R12.AC4)
183	  verified_version: "2.1.158"          # CLI it was confirmed against
184	  verified_date: "2026-05-29"
185	  unverified: false               # true ⇒ rendered with an explicit "unverified" marker (R12.AC3)
186	  notes: ""
187	```
188	
189	**Reference syntax (prose → version-data):** prose uses an inline token **`{{vd:key}}`** wherever a
190	version-specific value would otherwise be typed. The link-/reference-integrity check (R13.AC4, §8)
191	fails the build if a `{{vd:key}}` has no matching key. Because the token is plain text, an
192	unprocessed Markdown viewer still shows a legible placeholder (R15.AC2 graceful degradation).
193	
194	**Callout convention:** rendered version details use a labelled blockquote, meaning carried by **text**
195	(not color/emoji alone, R15.AC6):
196	
197	```markdown
198	> **⚙️ Version detail (CLI {{vd:_verified_version}}):** the permission modes are {{vd:permission-modes}}.
199	> *If your `claude --version` differs, see `meta/version-record.md`.* (R11.AC5)
200	```
201	
202	**Version record** — `meta/version-record.md`: a table of *last-verified CLI version* + *date* +
203	*method*, seeded at **2.1.158 / 2026-05-29** (R12.AC5). Bumped on every refresh (R12.AC7).
204	
205	**Drift-detection check** — `tools/check-version-drift` (§8): compares installed `claude --version`
206	to `version-record`; where feasible, diffs the current `claude --help` command list against a recorded
207	snapshot to surface new/removed/renamed commands (R12.AC6). Doubles as a hooks/CI worked example
208	(R14.AC2). **Hard rule (R12.AC3):** no `{{vd:*}}` value is authored from model memory — each is
209	verified against the installed CLI (`--help`/`/help`/docs) or marked `unverified: true`.
210	
211	**Bounded change surface (R12.AC8):** a CLI version bump touches principally `version-data.yaml`
212	(+ regen `.json`) and `version-record.md`; prose is principles-first and need not change.
213

276	## 6.5 Capstone design  → produces `course/capstone/`  [R8]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
277	
278	The single graded assessment (R8.AC1) — sole graded instrument; units stay instructional (R6.AC7).
279	
280	- **Brief menu (R8.AC2):** a small menu of choose-your-own briefs (≥3), each a realistic non-trivial
281	  end-to-end job requiring **context engineering + ≥1 custom extension (command/subagent/skill/hook/MCP)
282	  + a non-trivial workflow + explicit verification** (R8.AC1). Candidate briefs: (a) add a substantial
283	  feature to `taskflow-api` spec-first with a custom authoring command; (b) rescue/refactor a slice of
284	  `taskflow-cli` with a subagent + hook guardrail; (c) build a small MCP integration for the app and
285	  vet it. Briefs are a Design list, refined in P6.
286	- **Exemplar (R8.AC2 / R14.AC4):** the "how this course was built with Claude Code" **build case study**
287	  doubles as the worked exemplar so the learner never faces a blank page.
288	- **Rubric (R8.AC3/AC4):** dimensions collectively cover **every** can-do C1–C17 + CV; grades the
289	  **work product** per can-do as *demonstrated / partially / not yet* (never ranks the learner);
290	  **self-applicable** by a solo learner. Built from `meta/capability-map` so coverage is checkable (R13.AC5).
291	- **Optional AI-assisted self-grade (R8.AC5):** learner may have Claude score the capstone, then must
292	  **critique** that grade (trust-but-verify, CV/R10).
293	- **Verification reflection (R8.AC6):** required structured prompts — one catch, one justified accept,
294	  one override/redirect.
295	- **Optional mid-course checkpoint (R8.AC7):** ungraded self-scored challenge after the Daily-Driver
296	  stage; does not alter capstone-only status.

298	## 7. Lab & solution architecture  [R7]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
299	
300	**Primary — `codebases/primary/` (`taskflow-api`)** — FastAPI + SQLModel/SQLite, JWT auth; non-trivial
301	but tractable (~2–4k LOC), with a real **pytest** suite that starts green (R7.AC1/AC2):
302	
303	```
304	taskflow-api/
305	├─ app/
306	│  ├─ main.py                # FastAPI app + router wiring
307	│  ├─ api/routers/           # auth.py users.py projects.py tasks.py
308	│  ├─ models/                # SQLModel entities (User, Project, Task)
309	│  ├─ services/              # task/project domain logic
310	│  ├─ core/                  # config.py security.py (JWT, hashing)
311	│  └─ db/                    # session, migrations/seed
312	├─ tests/                    # pytest, fixtures, factory data
313	└─ pyproject.toml
314	```
315	Domain: projects contain tasks (status, assignee, due date); CRUD + filtering/pagination + auth.
316	Mostly clean; per-lab defects are introduced on lab branches, not baked into `main`.
317	
318	**Legacy — `codebases/legacy/` (`taskflow-cli`)** — a deliberately messy argparse CLI that "predates"
319	the API and stores tasks in a JSON file. Authentic accreted complexity for W5/W8 (R7.AC1): one
320	~800-line god-module, global mutable state, duplicated logic, inconsistent naming, dead code, **no
321	tests**, and a few real bugs (e.g., naive date parsing, off-by-one in listing, a swallowed exception).
322	Used by U7 (debug), U9 (onboard + refactor).
323	
324	**Lab format (R7.AC3):** each lab states **goal · starting state · steps/prompts · objective
325	self-check**. Conventions:
326	- **Starting state:** a tagged commit `start/uNN-labM` (clean, reproducible).
327	- **Reference solution (R7.AC4):** a branch `solution/uNN-labM` (inspectable diff), kept separate from
328	  the prompt so the learner attempts unaided first.
329	- **Automated verification (R7.AC5):** `tools/verify-lab.sh uNN-labM` runs the lab's check (e.g.
330	  `pytest -k`, or a script); where automation is infeasible, the prose self-check (R7.AC3) suffices.
331	- **Reset (R7.AC6):** `tools/reset-lab.sh uNN-labM` restores the `start/...` tag so the next lab begins
332	  clean.
333	- **Offline/mock (R7.AC7):** no required external service or credential; MCP/web labs ship a local mock
334	  server/fixtures so they run standalone.
335	- **BYO variant (R7.AC8):** optional stretch on the learner's own repo, clearly marked
336	  **non-verifiable** (no objective self-check) and never the required path.
337	
338	**Seeded-defect inventory:** `codebases/SEEDED.md` (maintainer-facing) maps each lab → its seeded
339	bug/smell and the expected fix, so defects are tracked, not silently scattered. Safety-fenced labs
340	(e.g. the U3 prompt-injection lab, R10.AC3/AC8) carry an explicit safety note + safe-by-default path.
341	
342	## 8. Tooling & enforcement  → produces `tools/`  [R11, R12, R13]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
343	
344	Implemented as a small **Python** `tools/` package (matches the codebases' stack), each check runnable
345	standalone and via `make check`. The checks are themselves the **worked examples** the hooks/CI units
346	reference (R14.AC2), so they must be real and clean.
347	
348	| Tool | Purpose | Req |
349	|---|---|---|
350	| `tools/doctor` (+ manual checklist fallback) | Preflight: install, version-floor, auth, a first command (pass/fail). Doubles as a scripting-Claude example. | R11.AC2 |
351	| `check-frontmatter` | Validate every unit's front matter against `unit-frontmatter.schema.json` | R13.AC4a |
352	| `check-coverage` | Every can-do (C1–C17+CV) **and** every coverage-matrix area is covered by ≥1 unit/lab | R13.AC4b |
353	| `check-links` | Internal/external link checking | R13.AC4c |
354	| `check-version-refs` | Every `{{vd:key}}` in prose resolves to a key in `version-data` | R13.AC4d |
355	| `check-traceability` | Every R-ID referenced by ≥1 artifact; every can-do → ≥1 lab **and** ≥1 rubric dimension | R13.AC5 |
356	| `check-version-drift` | Installed `claude --version` vs `version-record`; surface new/removed commands | R12.AC6 |
357	| `verify-lab` / `reset-lab` | Per-lab automated check + clean reset (see §7) | R7.AC5/AC6 |
358	
359	**Wiring (R13.AC6):** the same suite runs as **local hooks** (fast authoring feedback — Claude Code
360	hooks for in-session feedback + a git `pre-commit` hook) **and** in **CI** (GitHub Actions backstop
361	gate). `check-version-drift` additionally runs on a schedule/trigger to prompt refresh (R12.AC7).
362	Build order note: **P3 builds these before the content (P5)** so they guard authoring from day one.

376	├─ course/

378	│  ├─ capstone/                   # briefs, exemplar (build case study), rubric (§6.5)
379	│  ├─ progress-checklist.md       # generated from capability-map (R9.AC5)
380	│  ├─ stuck.md                    # "when you're stuck" recovery (R9.AC4)

387	│  ├─ capability-map.{yaml,json}  use-case-catalog.yaml  coverage-matrix.yaml
388	│  ├─ version-data.{yaml,json}  version-record.md  workflows.md
389	│  ├─ glossary.md  conventions.md  unit-frontmatter.schema.json  templates/

391	├─ .claude/                       # dogfooding: commands, skills, hooks, settings.json (R14)
392	└─ .github/workflows/             # CI backstop running the check suite (R13.AC6)
393	```
394	
395	**Naming conventions (R13.AC1):** units `course/units/NN-slug/` (zero-padded `NN`); meta files
396	kebab-case; lab tags/branches `start/uNN-labM` and `solution/uNN-labM`; tools kebab-case;
397	version-data keys kebab-case (`{{vd:key}}`). Documented in `meta/conventions.md`.
398	
399	**Accessibility & portability (R15):** author in **CommonMark** for core meaning; GitHub
400	callouts/Mermaid only as graceful-degrading enhancement (R15.AC1/AC2). Diagrams/images carry text
401	equivalents; **no meaning by color or emoji alone**; semantic headings/lists (R15.AC6). Labs are
402	**portable-by-default** (macOS/Linux, **WSL** as the v1 Windows story; native Windows out of scope),
403	platform-specific steps quarantined into marked sections (R15.AC3/AC4). No required lab depends on a
404	second paid service; Claude Code itself is the exempt prerequisite A1 (R15.AC5).

406	## 10. Dogfooding plan  [R14]  ✅ AUTHORED (2026-05-29)
407	
408	Only **authentic** artifacts — genuinely used to build/maintain the course, never props (R14.AC3).
409	Each is referenced by the unit that teaches its feature (R14.AC2), and references are kept accurate by
410	`check-links`/`check-version-refs` (R14.AC6):
411	
412	| Authentic artifact | Referenced by | Req |
413	|---|---|---|
414	| **This spec** (`specs/claude-code-mastery/`) | U10 spec-driven dev (the worked example) | R3.AC2, R14.AC1 |
415	| `CLAUDE.md` (project memory) | U4 memory & context | R14.AC1 |
416	| Course-authoring **command/skill** | U12 commands & skills; maintainer guide (R13.AC3) | R14.AC1 |
417	| Enforcement **hooks** + check suite (§8) | U14 hooks | R14.AC2 |
418	| `check-version-drift` | U12/U16 + U10 maintenance discussion | R12.AC6, R14.AC2 |
419	| **MCP** config + local mock | U15 MCP & vetting | R14.AC2 |
420	| `doctor` script | U1 onboarding | R11.AC2, R14.AC2 |
421	| CI workflow (`.github/workflows/`) | U16 automate & scale | R14.AC2 |
422	| **Decision log** (`decisions.md`) | maintainers; optional teaching material | R14.AC7 |
423	
424	- **Build case study (R14.AC4):** "how this course was built and is maintained with Claude Code"
425	  (spec-driven workflow + R12.AC7 refresh process) → lives in `course/capstone/` and **doubles as the
426	  capstone exemplar** (R8.AC2, §6.5).
427	- **AI-authorship transparency note (R14.AC5):** a short, honest disclosure that parts were authored
428	  with Claude Code, modeling the responsible-output guidance of R10.AC9.
429	
430	## 11. Traceability table  [§8 of requirements]
431	Fill as sections are completed — every R1–R15 → the design component(s) and artifact(s) that satisfy
432	it. The R13.AC5 check automates the inverse (every requirement referenced; every can-do → lab + rubric).
433	**Status:** ✅ = design authored; rows without a mark await their section.
434	
435	| Requirement | Design section(s) | Primary artifact(s) |
436	|---|---|---|
437	| R1 ✅ | §1, §6 | capability-map (C1–C17, CV), progress-checklist |
438	| R2 ✅ | §2 | use-case-catalog (U1–U16) |
439	| R3 ✅ | §3, §6, §7 | workflows.md (W1–W9), units, labs (all 9 mapped in §2) |
440	| R4 ✅ | §4 | coverage-matrix (29 areas seeded) |
441	| R5 ✅ | §6 | front-matter schema (time estimates, tiers), templates (fast-path/skip-check) |
442	| R6 ✅ | §6 | front-matter schema, core/awareness templates |
443	| R7 ✅ | §7 | codebases (taskflow-api/-cli), lab format, solution branches, reset, SEEDED.md |
444	| R8 ✅ | §6.5 | capstone brief menu, exemplar (build case study), self-applicable rubric |
445	| R9 ✅ | §6, §6.5 | dependency graph, progress-checklist (gen), stuck.md, self-applicable rubric |
446	| R10 ✅ | §2, §3, §6, §7 | security unit U3, injection lab (§7), woven CV verification (§1/§3) |
447	| R11 ✅ | §6, §8 | doctor (§8), onboarding unit U1, baseline config (R11.AC4) |
448	| R12 ✅ | §5, §8 | version-data, version-record (2.1.158), drift check, `{{vd:key}}` convention |
449	| R13 ✅ | §8, §9 | check + traceability suite, conventions, maintainer guide |
450	| R14 ✅ | §10 | dogfooding inventory, build case study, transparency note |
